departmentsSettings.infoBox2Text: The system creates default departments (Kitchen, Bar, Universal) automatically. You can add custom departments as needed for your restaurant's structure.
departmentsSettings.infoBox3Text: Staff members belong to one department. If someone needs access to multiple features, assign them to a department with broader permissions or create a custom department.

reportLoss.title: Report Stock Loss
reportLoss.subtitle: Mark items for removal and track losses
reportLoss.helpTextRecipe: Items with ingredients linked will have stock auto-deducted. Items marked "⚠️ No recipe" need ingredients added in Menu management.
reportLoss.successWithRecipe: Loss recorded successfully! {quantity} {itemName} marked for removal. Stock has been updated.

reportLoss.infoBullet1: Select any menu item that needs to be marked for removal
reportLoss.infoBullet2: Items with ingredients linked: Stock is automatically deducted
reportLoss.infoBullet3: Items without ingredients: Loss is logged but stock must be adjusted manually
reportLoss.infoBullet4: All losses are tracked with your name, timestamp, and reason
reportLoss.infoBullet5: View all losses in Analytics → Losses
reportLoss.tipTitle: 💡 Tip for single items (e.g., Coca Cola 330ml):
reportLoss.tipText: Add a single ingredient linking to the stock product with the serving size. This allows automatic stock deduction even for non-recipe items.
reportLoss.menuItemLossDesc: Log a prepared dish or drink
reportLoss.stockItemLossDesc: Log an ingredient or raw stock

reportLoss.stockInfoBullet2: Stock will be immediately deducted from current levels
reportLoss.stockInfoBullet3: The loss appears in the Stock Movement Report as Lost/Adj.

productTax.title: Product Tax Categories
productTax.subtitle: Create tax categories for your stock products. Tax will be applied when purchasing products.
productTax.menuSalesTaxInfo: Configure a single tax (VAT, GST, etc.) that applies to all menu items using tax-inclusive pricing.
productTax.stockProductTaxInfo: Assign different tax categories to stock products for purchase taxes (e.g., import duties, excise tax).
productTax.menuSalesTaxDesc: Configure the tax applied to menu items with tax-inclusive pricing
productTax.defaultFallbackDesc: Applied to any menu item that has no specific tax category assigned.
productTax.thisRateApplied: This rate will be applied to all menu items on invoices
productTax.menuSalesTaxCategoriesDesc: Create different tax brackets for your menu items (e.g. Beer 21%, Soft Drinks 17%, Food 9%). Assign them to individual items in the Menu page.
productTax.noMenuTaxCategories: No sales tax categories yet. Add one below.
productTax.stockProductTaxDesc: Create tax categories for stock products (applied when purchasing ingredients)
productTax.taxInclusiveInfo: Your menu prices already include tax. Customers pay exactly what's shown on the menu. Invoices will show the breakdown (net amount + tax) for accounting purposes.
productTax.noCategories: No tax categories yet. Add one below to get started.
productTax.categoryNamePlaceholder: e.g., Base Products, Alcohol, Sugary Products
productTax.pageSubtitle: Manage tax rates applied to your sales and stock purchases.
productTax.menuSalesTaxFullDesc: These are the tax rates applied to items when they are sold to customers. Each menu item can be assigned its own tax bracket (e.g. Beer at 21%, Soft Drinks at 17%, Food at 9%). Tax-inclusive pricing means customers pay the shown price — invoices then show the net + tax breakdown for accounting.
productTax.example: Example: {symbol}5.00 menu price with 21% VAT → Customer pays {symbol}5.00 → Invoice: Net {symbol}4.13 + VAT {symbol}0.87
productTax.stockProductTaxFullDesc: These are the tax rates applied to stock items when you purchase them as ingredients or supplies. Each stock product can be assigned a different tax category (e.g. excise duty on alcohol, import tax on specialty items). This helps accurately track your purchasing costs.

taxInvoicing.pageSubtitle: Configure invoice generation for corporate clients and tax purposes
taxInvoicing.accessError: Only restaurant owners can access settings.
taxInvoicing.sectionDescription: Configure invoice generation for corporate clients and tax purposes.
taxInvoicing.footerTextPlaceholder: Thank you for your business! Payment terms: Net 30 days.
taxInvoicing.footerTextHelp: This text will appear at the bottom of every invoice
taxInvoicing.requireSequentialDescription: Ensure invoices are numbered in order
taxInvoicing.requireCustomerVatDescription: Make VAT number mandatory for invoices
taxInvoicing.templateSectionDescription: Choose a template design for your invoices. This will apply to all generated invoices.
taxInvoicing.templateClassicDescription: Traditional business invoice with clean table design and professional layout
taxInvoicing.templateModernDescription: Sleek minimalist design with lots of white space and subtle borders
taxInvoicing.templateBoldDescription: Vibrant header with brand colors and eye-catching design
taxInvoicing.templateCompactDescription: Dense layout with smaller fonts to fit more information

security.dineInMenuDesc: Share this link to let customers browse your full menu online. Great for your website or social media.
security.dineInUrlDesc: Customers can view your full menu at this link:
security.dineInTip: This is your main menu page. Customers scanning table QR codes will see a version of this with table-specific features.
security.bookingPageDesc: Share this link to let customers book tables online. Perfect for your website, Google Business Profile, or social media.
security.bookingUrlDesc: Customers can book a table at your restaurant using this link:
security.bookingTip: Add this link to your website and Google Business Profile to make it easy for customers to book tables online.

loginSteps.step1: Staff scan the Install App QR code to download the app
loginSteps.step2: Or they scan the Login Page QR code to use in browser
loginSteps.step3: They enter the restaurant password (one-time per session)
loginSteps.step4: They enter their personal 3-digit PIN code

security.securityTip: Security tip: Change this password regularly and only share it with trusted staff members.

messages.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 6 characters

dataMigration.title: Data Migration
dataMigration.subtitle: Import your menu items or stock products from a CSV file.
dataMigration.accessDenied: Only restaurant owners can access data migration.
dataMigration.chooseType: What would you like to import?
dataMigration.chooseTypeDesc: Choose a data type to get started. You can run this tool multiple times.
dataMigration.menuItemsDesc: Import your full menu with prices and categories. Categories will be created automatically if they don't exist.
dataMigration.stockProductsDesc: Import your inventory products with current stock levels. Useful when moving from another stock management system.
dataMigration.step1Desc: Download our CSV template, fill it with your data, then upload it below. All columns except the name
dataMigration.stockBaseUnitDesc: ml for liquids, grams for solids (default: grams)
dataMigration.stockCurrentDesc: optional, numeric quantity in base units
dataMigration.stockValueDesc: optional, total estimated value of current stock (e.g. 36.00) — used to calculate average cost per unit
dataMigration.dragDrop: Drag and drop your CSV here
dataMigration.duplicateHandling: If an item with the same name already exists:
dataMigration.errorRowsTitle: Rows with errors (will not be imported)
dataMigration.noValidRows: No valid rows to import. Please fix the errors above and try again.
dataMigration.importCompleteDesc: Your data has been imported successfully.
dataMigration.exportDesc: Download your menu items or stock products as CSV — useful for backups or migrating to another system.
dataMigration.infoDesc: This tool imports menu items and stock products only. Historical orders, staff accounts, and floor plans are not migrated — those are set up fresh in Veno App. Ingredient recipes (linking stock to menu items) can be added manually after import from the Menu page.

support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Get help from our team
support.noTicketsSubtitle: Submit a ticket and our team will get back to you.
support.ticketClosedNote: This ticket is {status}. Open a new ticket if you need further help.

customers.title: Customers
customers.subtitle: {count} customers who have booked this venue
customers.noPeerReviews: No peer reviews found for this customer.

salesBalance.title: Sales & Tax Balance Report
salesBalance.subtitle: Products sold, recipe costs, and tax collected vs. purchase tax paid
salesBalance.noSalesFound: No sales found for this period.
salesBalance.taxBalanceNote: * Net tax payable = tax collected on sales minus tax paid on recipe ingredients. Consult your accountant for official VAT/tax returns.

stockMovement.title: Stock Movement Report
stockMovement.subtitle: Track stock usage, purchases, and losses across a time period
stockMovement.noStockItems: No stock items found for this filter.
stockMovement.infoNote: How this works: "Closing qty" is the live current quantity from your stock records. "Opening qty" is estimated as closing + used − purchased during the period. "Used in recipes" is calculated from paid orders × recipe ingredient quantities. "Lost/Adj." are negative stock entries (manual adjustments or recorded losses). Click any row to expand usage breakdown.

reservationSettings.title: Reservation Settings
reservationSettings.subtitle: Configure booking slots, hours and availability
reservationSettings.errorSave: Failed to save settings. Please try again.
reservationSettings.errorSelectDuration: Please select at least one duration option for customers.
reservationSettings.industryCategoryDesc: Your venue's category is assigned by the platform administrator and determines which peer reviews are shown for your customers.
reservationSettings.bookingSlotModeDesc: Choose whether customers select from fixed time slots you define, or whether they pick their own session duration.
reservationSettings.fixedSlotsDesc: You define the interval. Customers pick a start time from pre-made slots (e.g. every 30 min).
reservationSettings.customerChoosesDesc: You offer duration options. Customers first choose how long they need, then pick a start time.
reservationSettings.durationOptionsDesc: Select all that apply. Customers will choose from these before picking a time.
reservationSettings.bufferTimeDesc: Extra time after each confirmed booking before the next slot opens. Useful for cleaning, setup, or travel between sessions.
reservationSettings.bufferNote: After a confirmed booking, the next available slot will start {minutes} minute{plural} later.
reservationSettings.advanceBookingWindowDesc: How many days in advance customers can make a reservation.
reservationSettings.partySizeDesc: When enabled, customers choose how many people will attend. Disable if each booking is always for one person (e.g. individual appointments).
reservationSettings.partySizeOn: Party size selection shown to customers
reservationSettings.partySizeOff: Hidden — each booking is treated as 1 person
reservationSettings.singleBookingAreaDesc: Enable if your venue has only one bookable space (e.g. a private dining room, a studio, a single court). When enabled, only one booking can be confirmed per time slot — no table assignment needed. Pending bookings block the slot to prevent double-bookings.
reservationSettings.singleAreaOn: Single area — only one booking per slot, no table assignment required
reservationSettings.singleAreaOff: Multiple areas — table assignment required, multiple concurrent bookings allowed
reservationSettings.seeMenuButtonDesc: When enabled, a button is shown on the booking confirmation page linking customers to your menu. You can optionally override the button label below.
reservationSettings.menuButtonOn: Button visible on booking confirmation page
reservationSettings.operatingHoursDesc: Set open and close times for each day. Closed days are greyed out on the booking calendar.
reservationSettings.minAdvanceNoticeDesc: Customers must book at least this many days before their visit. Set to 0 to allow same-day bookings. This overrides operating hours — even if a day is open, it won't appear as bookable within this window.
reservationSettings.minAdvanceNote: Customers must book at least {n} day{plural} in advance. Today and the next {remaining} day{remainingPlural} will not be bookable.
reservationSettings.reservationFeeDesc: Charge every customer a booking fee when they reserve. Requires SMS verification and Stripe Connect to be active. When enabled, individual customer deposit requirements are overridden by this fee.
reservationSettings.smsRequired: SMS verification must be enabled to use a global reservation fee. Enable it in the SMS add-on settings.
reservationSettings.stripeRequired: Stripe Connect must be set up to collect booking fees. Complete onboarding in your billing settings.
reservationSettings.feeNote: All customers will be charged this fee during booking. Fully or partially blocked customers still cannot book.

billing.title: Billing & Subscription
billing.subtitle: Choose the modules your business needs.
billing.trialDaysLeft: {n} day{plural} left on your free trial
billing.trialExpires: Trial expires {date}. Subscribe below to keep full access.
billing.msgSuccess: Subscription activated! Your modules are now unlocked.
billing.msgCanceled: Checkout cancelled. No charge was made.
billing.msgUpdated: Subscription updated! Your modules have been changed.
billing.msgAtLeastOne: You must keep at least one module. To cancel entirely, use Manage Billing.
billing.msgGenericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
billing.trialActiveNoExpiry: Free trial active — no expiry date set
billing.paymentFailed: Payment failed — update your payment method to restore access.
billing.modulesActiveDesc: Toggle modules on or off, then click Update plan to apply changes.
billing.modulesTrialDesc: Pick one or more — 15% bundle discount applies when you choose 2 or more.
billing.removedModulesNote: Removed modules will remain accessible until the end of the current billing period.
billing.trialNote: All modules include a 14-day free trial. Cancel anytime. Prices exclude VAT. Payments processed securely by Stripe.
billing.smsRate: At {rate}p per SMS · added to your next invoice
billing.smsAddonDesc: Require customers to verify with their phone number. Enables ratings, blacklist & per-customer deposit fees.
billing.trialNoExpiry: Free trial active — no expiry date set
billing.pastDueNote: Payment failed — update your payment method to restore access.

settingsHub.title: Settings
settingsHub.subtitle: Manage {name}
settingsHub.restaurantDesc: Name, phone, logo and general info
settingsHub.taxInvoicingDesc: Tax system, rates, and invoice configuration
settingsHub.productTaxDesc: Assign tax rates to individual products
settingsHub.securityDesc: Staff login password and access control
settingsHub.reservationsDesc: Booking slots, operating hours and availability
settingsHub.paymentsDesc: Connect your Stripe account to collect booking deposits
settingsHub.dataMigrationDesc: Import or export your restaurant data
settingsHub.offlineHubDesc: Configure local WiFi hub for offline order relay

payments.title: Payments
payments.subtitle: Connect your Stripe account to collect booking deposits from customers. Deposits go directly to your bank account.
payments.notifIncomplete: Onboarding incomplete. Please try again to finish connecting your account.
payments.notifError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
payments.paymentsEnabled: Payments enabled — deposits go directly to your bank
payments.payoutsPending: Payouts pending — Stripe may need more info
payments.setupIncomplete: Your Stripe account setup is incomplete. Click below to finish.
payments.connectPrompt: Connect your Stripe account to start collecting booking deposits. Your bank details are entered directly with Stripe — we never see them.
payments.step1: You mark a customer as requiring a deposit in the reservations dashboard (blacklist → fee required)
payments.step2: When that customer books, they're asked to pay the deposit before their booking is accepted
payments.step3: The customer is informed the deposit will be deducted from their bill and is non-refundable if they don't show up
payments.step4: Funds land in your connected Stripe account and are paid out to your bank on Stripe's standard schedule
payments.cardReadersNone: No readers registered. Add readers in the Stripe Dashboard under Terminal → Readers.

guide.overview_desc: Your daily dashboard — see today's orders, pending items, and a snapshot of your restaurant's activity at a glance.
guide.orders_desc: View and manage incoming orders. New orders appear here in real time. Mark them as ready when prepared, or cancel if needed.
guide.orders_status_filter_desc: Filter orders by their current stage. Active shows orders still being prepared. Completed shows paid or fulfilled orders. All shows everything.
guide.orders_type_filter_desc: Filter by order type. Dine-in orders come from customers seated at a table. Takeaway orders are for collection.
guide.tables_desc: Set up your tables and generate QR codes customers scan to place orders. Each table gets its own unique code.
guide.floor_plan_desc: Drag and drop your tables to match your physical layout. Staff can view this to know where each table is.
guide.reservations_desc: See upcoming bookings, confirm or reject reservation requests, and track walk-ins alongside pre-booked guests.
guide.customers_desc: A record of all customers who have made reservations. Track visit history and contact details.
guide.menu_desc: Build and manage your menu. Organise items into categories, set prices, and control what's visible to customers.
guide.menu_items_desc: Add, edit, or remove individual dishes and drinks. Set names, descriptions, prices, and assign each item to a category.
guide.menu_categories_desc: Create the sections your menu is divided into (e.g. Starters, Mains, Desserts). Items are grouped under these.
guide.stock_desc: Track your ingredients and supplies. Record deliveries, monitor levels, and get alerts when stock runs low.
guide.stock_products_desc: Define the products and ingredients you stock. Each product can be linked to menu items to auto-deduct when orders are placed.
guide.stock_inventory_desc: Record current stock levels and run regular counts. Compare against expected levels to spot discrepancies.
guide.stock_invoices_desc: Log supplier invoices when stock arrives. Keeps a purchase history and helps reconcile deliveries against orders.
guide.report_loss_desc: Record when stock or menu items are wasted, spoiled, or lost. This feeds into your loss analytics.
guide.staff_rota_desc: Manage your team — add staff members, assign departments, and set their weekly schedule from here.
guide.my_rota_desc: Your personal work schedule. See your upcoming shifts at a glance.
guide.my_availability_desc: Let your manager know which days and times you're available to work each week.
guide.time_off_desc: Submit requests for days off. Your manager will approve or decline from their end.
guide.analytics_desc: Dive into your sales data — revenue trends, best-selling items, table turnover, and staff performance over time.
guide.reports_desc: Generate formal reports for accounting and reconciliation. Includes daily, weekly, monthly, and tax summaries.
guide.zreport_desc: End-of-day reconciliation report. Run this once at close to lock the day's totals — cash, card, and voids.
guide.xreport_desc: A snapshot of sales so far today without closing the day. Useful for mid-shift checks.
guide.support_desc: Raise issues or questions with the Menu Hub support team. Track open tickets and view responses here.
guide.settings_desc: Configure your restaurant — details, tax setup, payment methods, reservation rules, and staff permissions.
guide.cash_drawer_desc: Track cash in and out of the till. Open sessions at the start of a shift and close them at the end.
guide.discounts_desc: Create fixed or percentage discounts. Apply them manually at checkout or set them to trigger automatically.
guide.departments_desc: Define the departments in your restaurant (e.g. Kitchen, Bar, Front of House) and set what each one can access.
guide.billing_desc: View your current subscription plan, manage payment details, and see your billing history.
guide.payments_desc: Connect and configure your payment methods — Stripe, cash, card readers, and split billing options.
guide.reports_weekly_desc: A week-by-week breakdown of revenue, orders, labour costs, and discounts. Useful for spotting trends and comparing performance.
guide.reports_monthly_desc: A full month summary covering revenue, costs, profit margin, and order volume. Useful for accounting and monthly reviews.
guide.reports_tax_desc: See a breakdown of tax collected across a date range, split by tax rate. Use this to prepare VAT or sales tax filings.
guide.reports_sales_balance_desc: A detailed reconciliation of all sales, payments, and refunds across a period. Use this to balance your books and verify takings.
guide.reports_stock_movement_desc: A log of all stock changes — deliveries, usage, adjustments, and losses — over a selected period. Useful for auditing and waste review.
guide.analytics_overview_desc: A bird's-eye view of your restaurant's performance — revenue trends, top-selling items, peak hours, and category breakdowns.
guide.analytics_tables_desc: See how your tables are performing — average spend per cover, turnover rate, and which tables generate the most revenue.
guide.analytics_staff_desc: Performance breakdown by staff member — orders handled, upsell rates, shift hours, and productivity over time.
guide.analytics_labor_desc: Compare labour costs against revenue to understand your wage efficiency. See hours worked, cost per hour, and labour-to-revenue ratio.
guide.analytics_losses_desc: Track all reported losses across menu items and stock. See patterns, totals by category, and compare against expected usage.
guide.analytics_losses_menu_desc: A breakdown of menu items marked as wasted or voided. Shows quantity, cost, and trends over your selected period.
guide.analytics_losses_stock_desc: A breakdown of stock items written off or reported as lost. Helps identify which products are being wasted most.
guide.restaurant_info_desc: Update your restaurant name, logo, contact details, address, and email language preference.
guide.tax_invoicing_desc: Configure your invoicing settings — enable digital invoices, set your tax system (VAT/Sales Tax), add tax rates, and customise the invoice template.
guide.product_tax_desc: Assign tax categories to individual menu items and stock products. Useful when different items attract different tax rates.
guide.security_desc: Set a staff login password, generate a staff login QR code, and manage connected devices that can access this dashboard.
guide.reservation_settings_desc: Configure how online reservations work — set opening hours, booking rules, guest limits, and automatic confirmation behaviour.
guide.other_options_desc: Miscellaneous settings — notification sounds, order auto-accept behaviour, and other feature toggles.
guide.data_migration_desc: Import your existing menu items, categories, or stock products from a CSV file to quickly populate your account.

guidePage.title: Restaurant Guide
guidePage.subtitle: Step-by-step walkthroughs for the key processes in your restaurant.
guidePage.flow1_step2: Customer places order on their phone
guidePage.flow1_step3: Order appears on Orders page in real time
guidePage.flow1_step4: Kitchen / bar marks items as ready
guidePage.flow1_step6: Customer pays at table or counter
guidePage.flow2_step4: Add dishes: name, description, price, photo
guidePage.flow2_step5: Assign each item to a category
guidePage.flow2_step6: Customers see the live menu via QR scan
guidePage.flow3_step4: Set their permissions in Settings → Departments
guidePage.flow3_step5: Add their shifts in Staff → Rota
guidePage.flow3_step6: They log in using the staff PIN or QR code
guidePage.flow4_step3: Go to Cash Drawer → count physical cash
guidePage.flow4_step6: Run Z-Report to lock the day's figures
guidePage.flow5_step1: Customer makes a booking (online or phone)
guidePage.flow5_step6: Their visit is logged in Customers
